_validator: scripts/validate_topology.py — run `python3 scripts/validate_topology.py`. JSON Schema alone does NOT check referential integrity / group-edgelessness / fork-copy out-edge / version-line separation / constraints-not-pointing-at-groups; the validator does (see schema x-graph-invariants).

_readme.purpose: Machine-readable dependency-topology model of Mantle's active repos and their upstreams. Designed for agent/programmatic consumption (load, traverse, query 'what breaks if X updates'). Companion to the human-facing docs/outputs/mantle-upstream-topology-full.md (same topology as mermaid + prose).
node_model: Two node granularities. (1) GROUP nodes (is_group=true) represent a repo that has multiple version lines; they carry a 'lines' array of line-node ids and have NO edges — they exist only for display/grouping. (2) LINE nodes (member_of=<group id>) are the real graph nodes that edges attach to.

Rule enforced: 'same repo name + different ref/copy = different node' (matches mantle-upstream-topology-full.md §5/§10). So paradigmxyz/reth -> {paradigmxyz-reth-v2.2.0, paradigmxyz-reth-v1.6.0}; mantle-xyz/revm -> {mantle-revm-elysium, mantle-revm-v2.2.2}.
op-alloy is genuinely two code copies in two locations -> two independent nodes (op-alloy-standalone, op-alloy-mv2-subtree), each with its own consumer edges. Never merge.
Repos with a single version line stay as one ordinary node (no group/line split).
traversal_rules: Edges are directed UPSTREAM -> DOWNSTREAM. A->B means 'A is depended on / influences B'; an update in A propagates to B.

Blast radius of an upstream X = transitive closure of outgoing edges from X. ALWAYS start from a LINE node or an ordinary node, NEVER from a GROUP node (groups have no edges). To expand a group, read its 'lines' and traverse each line node separately — this prevents mixing unrelated version lines (e.g. reth v2.2.0 line vs kona v1.6.0 line).
edge.impact = severity on the downstream; edge.trigger tells whether Mantle must act (active-follow) or merely inherits (passive-inherit).

constraints[] are NON-dependency relationships (alignment / lockstep / dual-write / semantic-consistency). Convert them to an upgrade checklist via each constraint's required_actions + validation_queries; 'affects' lists the consumer nodes put at risk if the constraint is violated.
source_of_truth_priority: 1. Cargo.lock / go.sum resolved 'source = git+...#<sha>' (true pin) -> edge.via.resolved_sha
2. Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies] + [patch.*] / go.mod require+replace (intent; may contain vestigial declarations)

3. In-repo docs (MANTLE_CHANGES.md baseline table), header comments ('pinned by upstream X'), git merge-base / merge commits for vendored/subtree/merge provenance -> edge.via.merge_base / subtree_baseline / source_file

node.kind.external-upstream: Repo outside Mantle's control (root of an upstream chain)
node.kind.op-hub: OP-ecosystem monorepo that is the central hub feeding multiple Mantle repos
node.kind.bridge: Subtree relay repo used to sync a monorepo subtree into a Mantle repo
node.kind.mantle-fork: Mantle-maintained fork of an external lib (injects fee model / protocol changes, or re-points deps)
node.kind.mantle-fork-copy: A second divergent copy of a Mantle fork living inside another repo (distinct version line)
node.kind.mantle-repo: Mantle platform / consumer repo (the things being shipped)

_legend.node.is_group: true = display-only grouping node for a multi-version-line repo; has a 'lines' array and NO edges. Traverse its line nodes instead.
_legend.node.member_of: For line nodes: the id of the group node this version line belongs to.

node.tier.1: OP-ecosystem hub (the two monorepos + superchain-registry + bridge)

edge.type.vendored: Source copied in-tree (not a git dep); tracked by re-vendoring on new upstream tag
edge.type.git-merge: In-tree fork synced by periodic git merge / reset-to-baseline of an upstream branch or tag
edge.type.go-replace: go.mod 'replace' redirect to a Mantle fork
edge.type.subtree: Directory synced via git subtree pull (often through a bridge repo)
edge.type.cargo-pin: Cargo git/registry dependency pinned by tag/branch/rev in [workspace.dependencies]
edge.type.cargo-patch: Cargo [patch.crates-io] redirect (forces whole-graph unification of that crate)
edge.type.registry: Plain crates.io / go-module registry dependency (semver, no fork)

edge.role.bump-driver: The upstream Mantle actively follows (1st-class). Drives the upgrade cadence.
edge.role.inherited: Version inherited from a bump-driver's manifest, not independently chosen.
edge.role.mantle-fork: A Mantle-controlled fork (Mantle decides the ref).
edge.role.kernel: Underlying kernel inherited through an intermediate fork.

edge.trigger.active-follow: Mantle proactively bumps (re-vendor / reset baseline / merge / re-pin).
edge.trigger.passive-inherit: Moves only when the bump-driver moves.
edge.trigger.mantle-controlled: Mantle owns the fork ref and decides when to bump.
edge.trigger.version-pin: Registry version number, controllable via cargo/go update.

edge.via.ref_kind: What kind of provenance anchors this edge: tag/branch/rev/exact-version/merge-base/subtree-baseline/baseline-tag/kernel-inherited/fork-lineage/whole-repo-fork. Use fork-lineage / whole-repo-fork (+ provenance) for edges with NO resolvable pin, rather than putting prose into tag/baseline_tag.
edge.via.provenance: Free-text provenance note for edges whose anchor is not a resolvable ref (paired with ref_kind=fork-lineage/whole-repo-fork)
edge.via.ref: Human-readable ref label (tag/branch/rev as written; prefer a resolvable value)
edge.via.rev: git rev/commit pinned in manifest (machine field)
edge.via.resolved_sha: commit SHA resolved in the consumer's lockfile (string, or object keyed by consumer when it differs per consumer)
edge.via.subtree_baseline: upstream tag the subtree was pulled at
edge.via.kernel_version: inherited kernel version for kernel edges
edge.via.package_count: number of resolved packages this edge brings in
edge.via.source_file: in-repo file that documents this provenance

constraint.type.alignment: Two repos must resolve the SAME fork ref (same workspace forbids divergence).
constraint.type.lockstep: Two crates' versions must move together (e.g. revm major <-> revm-inspectors minor).
constraint.type.dual-write: A change must be landed in two separate copies to keep both consumers consistent.
constraint.type.semantic-consistency: Same protocol semantics implemented independently across stacks; one upgrade must land in all.

constraint.fields.participants: Nodes that hold the invariant (the things that must stay in sync)
constraint.fields.affects: Downstream consumer nodes put at risk if the invariant is violated
constraint.fields.required_actions: Upgrade-time checklist to keep the invariant
constraint.fields.validation_queries: Concrete checks an agent can run to verify the invariant holds

nodes.label: ethereum/go-ethereum
nodes.note: Ethereum kernel; Mantle sees it only through op-geth. Kernel version at Mantle baseline = v1.16.5.

nodes.label: bluealloy/revm
nodes.note: EVM core; fork source of mantle-xyz/revm (both version lines).

nodes.label: alloy-rs/op-alloy
nodes.note: OP consensus/RPC types; fork source of BOTH op-alloy copies (standalone 0.23.0 line and mantle-v2 subtree 2.0.0 line).

nodes.label: alloy-rs/evm
nodes.note: EVM abstraction (alloy-evm / alloy-op-evm); fork source of mantle-xyz/evm. NOTE: reth uses crates.io alloy-evm 0.34.0 upstream UNPATCHED, not this fork.

nodes.label: paradigmxyz/revm-inspectors
nodes.note: Tracing inspectors; fork source of mantle-xyz/revm-inspectors. Upstream main at 0.41.1; Mantle fork at 0.39.0.

nodes.label: paradigmxyz/reth (group)
nodes.note: GROUP node (no edges). TWO unrelated version lines reach different consumers; traverse the line nodes.

nodes.label: paradigmxyz/reth @ v2.2.0 (rev 88505c7f)
nodes.scope: full reth execution framework (~102 pkgs); global execution-layer bedrock
nodes.chosen_by: op-reth v2.2.1 manifest (Mantle inherits, does not choose)
nodes.note: reth line. rev = 88505c7fcbfdebfd3b56d88c86b62e950043c6c4.

nodes.label: paradigmxyz/reth @ v1.6.0
nodes.scope: reth-db / reth-codecs only (~19 pkgs); supervisor MDBX storage
nodes.note: kona line. Resolved sha d8451e54. Unrelated to the v2.2.0 line.

nodes.label: crates.io (alloy 全家 + precompile/crypto)
nodes.note: alloy base libs + precompile/crypto deps. Both VERSIONS and the dependency SET drift per consumer ref; resolve via each repo's own Cargo.lock. manifest semver is only a lower bound.

nodes.label: Succinct SP1 zkVM (sp1-* =6.1.0) + sp1-patches/*
nodes.note: zkVM runtime + proving system, pinned with exact '=6.1.0' (Hypercube). sp1-patches provide zkVM-accelerated crypto. UNIQUE to op-succinct. Any bump forces full ELF rebuild + possible verifying-key change.

nodes.label: succinctlabs/op-succinct
nodes.note: Proof harness; fork source of mantle-xyz/op-succinct. Mantle baseline merge-base = 530df86 (#555).

nodes.label: ethereum-optimism/optimism (monorepo)

feeds.rust/kona: future bump baseline for mantle-kona (op-rs/kona archived 2026-01-15)
feeds.rust/ subtree: subtree source for mantle-v2/rust (tag kona-client/v1.5.1 @ fbbf9089)
feeds.Go: whole-repo fork base for mantle-v2 (module path preserved)

nodes.note: THE Rust+protocol root. Also the home of op-reth and (post-archive) kona.

nodes.label: ethereum-optimism/op-geth (optimism branch)
nodes.note: OP execution layer (Go). Branch HEAD already at geth 1.17.2; Mantle baseline (merge-base 737ffd1bf) at 1.16.5 -> next bump crosses a minor.

nodes.label: ethereum-optimism/superchain-registry
nodes.note: Chain registry data.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/optimism-rust-bridge
nodes.note: Relays optimism rust/ subtree (split + sync) for mantle-v2 to subtree-pull. Tracks kona-client/v1.5.1.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/revm (group)
nodes.fork_form: leaf fork (published crates have NO Mantle internal deps)
nodes.role_in_stack: Root upstream of the Rust stack; single source of truth for Mantle fee model (BVM_ETH / token_ratio / ARSIA / JOVIAN). Largest blast-radius single point.
nodes.note: GROUP node (no edges). Two version lines reach disjoint consumers; traverse line nodes.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/revm @ branch mantle-elysium (revm 38)
nodes.note: elysium line. Consumers resolve different SHAs (reth b4f61822, mantle-v2 e637f61e). reth/mantle-v2 patch -> whole-graph unified.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/revm @ tag v2.2.2 (revm 31.0.2)
nodes.note: v2.2.2 line. kona does NOT patch -> its lock has registry revm-* AND git revm-* coexisting.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/op-alloy (standalone)
nodes.fork_form: near-root fork (crates.io alloy semver, no git pin; no Mantle internal deps)
nodes.note: ONE of two op-alloy copies. Consumed by kona + op-succinct via tag v2.2.0 @ 769c12a9. See dual-write constraint with op-alloy-mv2-subtree.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/evm
nodes.note: Consumed by kona + op-succinct (tag v2.2.1 @ ea9e2a86). reth does NOT use this fork (uses crates.io alloy-evm 0.34.0 unpatched).

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/revm-inspectors
nodes.fork_form: dependency re-pointing fork (only real delta: revm dep -> mantle-xyz/revm; no Mantle protocol logic)
nodes.role_in_stack: Middle node: consumes mantle-revm (elysium), consumed by mantle-reth (tracing/debug RPC only).
nodes.note: Consumed only by reth (in analyzed scope), via [workspace.dependencies] git fork + [patch.crates-io], branch mantle-elysium @ af522533. mantle-v2 declares registry 0.39.0 but does NOT use it (0 lock entries).

nodes.label: mantle-v2/rust/op-alloy (subtree copy)
nodes.fork_form: subtree copy inside mantle-v2 (distinct version line)
nodes.note: SECOND op-alloy copy, tracks a DIFFERENT alloy baseline than standalone (2.0.x vs 1.1.x). Consumed by reth via mantle-v2 branch mantle-elysium @ c06cb723 (see its own out-edge to mantle-reth).

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/op-geth (a.k.a. mantlenetworkio/op-geth)
nodes.fork_form: Go monolith fork (module = github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum; zero go.mod replace)
nodes.role_in_stack: Go execution-layer root. Mantle fee model + MetaTx + BVM_ETH + hardforks + preconf all written in-tree.
nodes.note: Upgrade via git merge of upstream/optimism (merge-base 737ffd1bf). Conflict-prone areas: core/types, core/txpool, core/vm, internal/ethapi.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/mantle-v2
nodes.fork_form: whole-repo OP monorepo fork (Go) + rust/ subtree
nodes.analyzed_ref: branch mantle-elysium @ afc419536 (rust deps stable since c06cb723)
nodes.role_in_stack: MIDDLE node: downstream of optimism+op-geth+revm, upstream of reth. Dual ecosystem (Go 27 op-* services + Rust subtree).

ecosystems.rust: kona (v1.5.1) + op-alloy(2.0.0) + alloy-op-evm + alloy-op-hardforks; only git dep is mantle-xyz/revm (patch)

nodes.note: Provides alloy-op-evm/alloy-op-hardforks to reth directly; provides op-alloy(2.0.0) to reth via the contained op-alloy-mv2-subtree node. Declares paradigmxyz/reth but lock has 0 refs (vestigial).

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/kona
nodes.fork_form: full repo fork (all kona-* are in-tree path members)
nodes.role_in_stack: Rust derivation + fault-proof stack. Also the upstream of op-succinct.
nodes.note: Mantle changes span protocol/* + proof/* + bin/{host,client} (63 files). Distinct from mantle-v2/rust/kona (v1.5.1, blob sources removed).

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/reth
nodes.fork_form: thin composition workspace (vendored op-reth + mantle-reth custom layer + patches)
nodes.note: Inherits paradigmxyz/reth rev (v2.2.0) from op-reth (does not choose it). Mantle fee patches in vendored op-reth/ (9 [MANTLE] files) + patches/reth-trie-common.

nodes.label: mantle-xyz/op-succinct
nodes.fork_form: fork + dependency re-pointing (thin Mantle adapter; heavy lifting in mantle-kona)
nodes.role_in_stack: MOST DOWNSTREAM: SP1 zkVM validity-proof system. Runs kona derivation+execution inside zkVM.
nodes.note: Re-points kona->mantle-xyz/kona v2.2.3; directly pins revm/op-alloy/evm but their refs MUST align with kona (same workspace). Removed Celestia/EigenDA DA backends.

constraints.rule: op-succinct and kona share one cargo workspace resolution; the revm(v2.2.2)/op-alloy(v2.2.0)/evm(v2.2.1) refs MUST be byte-identical, else workspace resolution conflicts. op-succinct directly pins them but cannot bump independently of kona.
required_actions: When bumping mantle-kona to a new tag, re-pin mantle-op-succinct's revm/op-alloy/evm to the SAME tags kona resolves
Rebuild op-succinct ELFs after any such bump
validation_queries: Compare the resolved git SHAs of revm/op-alloy/evm in mantle-kona/Cargo.lock vs mantle-op-succinct/Cargo.lock — must be byte-identical (currently revm 56aee9a6 / op-alloy 769c12a9 / evm ea9e2a86)

constraints.rule: revm major bump (e.g. 38->41) requires revm-inspectors bump to the matching upstream minor (revm 38 <-> inspectors 0.39, revm 41 <-> 0.41). reth reaches revm via two paths (direct patch + through inspectors); a mismatch yields two incompatible revm crates in reth's graph.
required_actions: When bumping mantle-revm (elysium) major, bump mantle-revm-inspectors to the matching upstream minor in the same change
Verify reth resolves a single revm version across both paths
validation_queries: grep mantle-reth/Cargo.lock for the revm crate — there must be exactly one resolved version; check it matches the revm-inspectors-pulled revm

constraints.rule: op-alloy is two divergent copies. A consensus/RPC change (eth_value/eth_tx_value/token_ratio) must be landed in BOTH (mantle-xyz/op-alloy for kona/op-succinct AND mantle-v2/rust/op-alloy for reth), else consumers see inconsistent op-alloy types. The copies also track different alloy baselines (1.1.x vs 2.0.x).
required_actions: Land any op-alloy consensus/RPC field change in BOTH mantle-xyz/op-alloy and mantle-v2/rust/op-alloy
Reconcile the differing alloy baselines (1.1.x standalone vs 2.0.x subtree) when porting
validation_queries: Diff TxDeposit (eth_value/eth_tx_value) and L1BlockInfo (token_ratio) field sets between mantle-xyz/op-alloy and mantle-v2/rust/op-alloy
Confirm kona/op-succinct (standalone copy) and reth (subtree copy) agree on op-alloy consensus types

semantics: Mantle DA blob format (Rust derive side)
constraints.rule: Mantle's hardfork + fee semantics are implemented INDEPENDENTLY on the Go stack (op-geth, carried by mantle-v2 Go services) and the Rust stack (revm/kona/op-alloy/reth). One hardfork upgrade must land in all of them in sync. This is NOT a code dependency edge but an upgrade-time invariant.
required_actions: Land each Mantle hardfork (Everest/Skadi/Limb/Arsia) in op-geth params AND kona genesis/hardforks AND revm op-revm, with identical activation timestamps
Propagate to mantle-v2 (Go services run op-geth; Rust subtree carries kona/op-alloy) and downstream reth/op-succinct
validation_queries: Compare hardfork activation timestamps in op-geth params/mantle.go (MantleUpgradeChainConfig) vs kona crates/protocol/genesis/.../mantle_hardfork.rs
Confirm op-revm ARSIA/JOVIAN gating matches the activation times used by op-geth and kona
